title: Otis: The Champ of Chubby Bears
story: Otis the bear loves to eat, and it shows! On October 5, 2021, this hefty hero won Alaska's Fat Bear Week contest for the third year running. Every autumn, brown bears in Katmai National Park gobble up salmon to prepare for winter sleep. Otis outpaced the competition, packing on pounds to become the chubbiest bear of them all. His impressive weight gain helps him survive the long, cold months ahead.
activity_question: What contest did Otis win in 2021?
bonus_fact: Bears can lose up to a third of their body weight during hibernation.

title: Banksy's Self-Shredding Surprise
story: A painting pulled off the ultimate prank! On October 5, 2018, Banksy's 'Girl With Balloon' sold for a million pounds at an auction in London. But seconds after the hammer fell, something shocking happened. The artwork began to shred itself! A hidden device in the frame turned the painting into strips. Banksy had planned this sneaky surprise all along. The half-shredded artwork was renamed 'Love is in the Bin', becoming an instant sensation.

Her fierce talent would lead her to join the famous reggae group Black Uhuru. Puma's soulful singing helped the band win hearts and awards during her time with them from 1979 to 1986.
activity_question: What was Sandra Jones' stage name?
bonus_fact: Reggae music originated in Jamaica in the late 1960s and quickly spread worldwide.

title: Sax Star of Madness Born
story: Saxophones wailed as Lee Thompson entered the world in 1957. Born in St Pancras, London, he'd grow up to be the sax player for the band Madness. Known as 'Kix', Thompson's funky tunes on hits like 'Baggy Trousers' and 'Our House' would make fans dance for decades. His October 5 birthday marked the start of a musical journey that brought joy to many.
activity_question: What nickname did Lee Thompson have?
bonus_fact: Madness blended ska and pop music styles.

title: From Navy to NASA: Brent Jett's Journey
story: Brent Jett blasted off into an amazing life on October 5, 1958. Born in Pontiac, Michigan, he grew up to become both a skilled naval officer and a brave NASA astronaut. Jett's incredible career took him from sailing the seas to soaring through space on the STS-72 mission. His journey shows how determination can lead to extraordinary adventures!
activity_question: What two careers did Brent Jett have?
follow_up_question: What skills do you think are important for both naval officers and astronauts?

title: A Game Show Legend Is Born
story: Lights, camera, action! Allen Ludden, the future king of game shows, entered the world in Mineral Point, Wisconsin. Born in 1919 on October 5, Ludden would grow up to become a beloved TV personality. He's best known for hosting the popular word game 'Password'. Fun fact: Ludden later married famous actress Betty White!
activity_question: What popular game show did Allen Ludden host?
follow_up_question: Can you think of a fun password game to play with your friends?

title: A Cartoonist's Circle of Life
story: Pencils and paper were Bil Keane's playground. Born in Philadelphia on October 5, 1922, he grew up to create 'The Family Circus,' a beloved comic strip. Keane's round panels captured the funny, heartwarming moments of family life, delighting readers for decades. His characters became like friends to millions, sharing laughter and love in newspapers across America.
activity_question: What famous comic strip did Bil Keane create?
bonus_fact: Bil Keane's son Jeff now continues 'The Family Circus,' keeping the family tradition alive.
